title: tragopogon × mirus
intro: Tragopogon × mirus, also known as the 'Esper's cypress-leaved fig marigold', is a sterile hybrid plant of the Tragopogon genus. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The roots of the plant are said to have diuretic properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and promoting urination, while its leaves have been traditionally used to treat colds, coughs, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the roots of Tragopogon × mirus are often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
